claim: Transformers generate text one token at a time, and sampling strategies affect the quality and diversity of outputs.
actionable: Understand token-by-token generation in transformers and experiment with sampling parameters to control output diversity.
source_title: The Ultimate Transformer Course for Working Engineers

claim: Hallucinations occur in LLMs due to how transformers and attention mechanisms work; techniques like RAG and constrained generation help reduce them.
actionable: Combat hallucinations by implementing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) or constrained generation to ground outputs in factual data.
source_title: The Ultimate Transformer Course for Working Engineers

claim: Inference optimizations like quantization, KV caching, flash attention, and speculative decoding improve transformer performance on GPUs.
actionable: Apply these optimization techniques when deploying transformers to improve inference speed and reduce memory usage.
source_title: The Ultimate Transformer Course for Working Engineers

claim: Positional encoding and attention mechanisms are key internal components that determine how transformers process sequences.
actionable: Study positional encoding and attention mechanisms to understand how transformers maintain context and meaning across long sequences.
source_title: The Ultimate Transformer Course for Working Engineers
